Details: Removal of mercury from water by membrane filtration (specifically ultrafiltration) can be very effective (15 percent to greater than 99 percent removal). Membrane filtration is not a a Best Available Technology for control of mercury; however, hollow fiber UF membranes appear to perform well in preliminary bench-scale testing. Microfiltration membranes used to filter laboratory samples containing mercury were also shown to remove mercury in small amounts (22 percent). In this test, the author indicated that the removal was likely due to the charges on the membrane.
